name: Shoreline v5
tagline: Full ground-up rebuild — multi-facility SaaS for senior living operations
description: Shoreline v5 is a complete rewrite of v4 — same purpose, entirely new architecture. It takes everything that worked in v4 and rebuilds it properly as a modern multi-tenant SaaS that could scale to hundreds of facilities. React 18, TypeScript, Vite, PostgreSQL, and a Next.js SaaS layer with full billing and team management.
forWho: Senior living operators and dietary managers who need v4's functionality but with a modern, cloud-based, multi-facility system. Also built as a template for what a proper care-facility SaaS should look like.
story: v4 worked. It solved real problems for real people. But it was built with duct tape and determination — vanilla JS, no types, hard to extend. When I leveled up as a developer, I looked back at Shoreline and knew it deserved a proper rebuild. v5 isn't just an upgrade — it's me applying everything I've learned to the project that started my serious development journey.
details: React 18 + Vite + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS frontend. PostgreSQL database with Prisma ORM. Next.js SaaS layer handles multi-tenancy, billing, and org management. Hosted on Railway. Full feature parity with v4 as the baseline, then extended with proper API design and real-time capabilities.
detailedSpecs: Multi-tenancy enforced at the Prisma query layer via a global middleware that injects facility_id into every read and write — no application code can accidentally leak cross-tenant data. Schema designed with facilities, residents, dietary_profiles, menu_cycles, production_worksheets, maintenance_tickets, shifts, and staff tables. Dietary profile model preserves full IDDSI texture classification (8 levels), allergy registry with cross-contamination flags, supplement tracking with administration schedule and route. Production worksheet generation is a server action that joins the menu cycle to the census count, applies portion sizes by diet type, and outputs a structured JSON worksheet renderable as a print-optimized page. Auth via NextAuth.js with credential + magic-link strategies; session carries facility_id and role claims. Billing with Stripe per-facility seat licensing. Hosted on Railway with auto-deploy from main; PostgreSQL managed instance on Railway with daily backups.
